title: zzop DSL rule pack v1
description: The DSL rule-pack contract (docs/rules/dsl-reference.md; crates/core/src/dsl/def.rs). Field names, required-ness, and defaults below mirror the Rust serde types field-for-field: a field is 'required' here exactly when its Rust struct field has neither #[serde(default)] nor an Option type; DSL pack fields are snake_case (packs are DSL-authored data files, not part of the camelCase JS-facing config boundary). Machine-check a pack without a schema validator via the validate_rule_pack MCP tool / `zzop pack validate <path>` — both run the engine's own loader judgments (structure only, never rule-quality semantics).
$comment: TOLERANCE CONTRACT: additionalProperties is true at every object level — the engine's serde derive silently ignores fields it does not recognize (no deny_unknown_fields anywhere in dsl/def.rs), same tolerant-input philosophy as the envelope schema (docs/adapters/envelope.schema.json). A pack authored against a FUTURE additive field still loads on an older engine. Only an incompatible revision of the matcher/pack shape itself bumps schema_version (see pack_loader::SUPPORTED_DSL_SCHEMA_VERSION's gate).

id.description: Pack id — the prefix of every finding's ruleId (`<pack id>/<rule id>`) and the unit of same-id collision replacement across packDefs/packsDir layers.

framework.description: Declared target environment ("any" | "react" | "prisma" | ...). Currently informational — carried on RuleMeta.framework, but no engine code path filters on it today (see docs/rules/dsl-reference.md's Pack shape table). Default "any".

schema_version.description: DSL schema version this pack was authored against. Defaults to 1 when absent; the loader rejects a pack whose version exceeds the engine's SUPPORTED_DSL_SCHEMA_VERSION (currently 1) rather than silently misinterpreting newer fields.

rules.description: The pack's rules, evaluated in array order.

id.description: Rule id — combined with the pack id as `<pack id>/<rule id>` in findings.

suppress_marker.description: Inline ok-marker: a `// <marker>` comment on the finding's line or the line above suppresses it (`-- <marker>` in .sql files). Absent or null = no inline suppression.

matcher.description: Dispatched on the `type` tag — one of the four matcher kinds (zzop_core::dsl::Matcher, kebab-case). line-scan/method-scan read source text (never run in envelope mode); symbol-scan/io-scan query the projected IR (run everywhere).

pattern.description: Regex (Rust `regex` crate syntax — no lookaround/backreferences). A non-compiling pattern silently no-ops its rule; `zzop pack validate` names it.

label.description: Classification label — lands in the finding's `data.label` (line-scan `any`) or names the pattern for `trigger`/veto roles (method-scan).

type.description: Per-line regex scan over source text. Use either `line_pattern` (single) or `any` (labeled alternatives, first match per line wins).

file_pattern.description: Target file-path regex (e.g. `(?i)\.(java|jsp)$`) — the rule only scans matching files.

require_file.description: Cheap pre-skip: only scan a file whose whole text matches this regex. Absent = always scan.

require_file_all.description: Additional pre-skip regexes, ALL of which must match the file text (short-circuits on first miss).

require_file_absent.description: Negated pre-skip: if ANY of these matches the whole file text, the rule skips the file entirely ("flag X only when there is no Y anywhere in the file").

skip_comment_lines.description: Skip lines whose trimmed start begins with `//`, `*`, or `/*`.

line_pattern.description: Regex that flags a line (single pattern, no label). Mutually alternative with `any`.

any.description: Labeled alternatives — first match per line wins, its label lands in the finding's `data.label`.

exclude_pattern.description: A line matching the main pattern is skipped when it ALSO matches this regex (per-line veto).

file_exclude_pattern.description: Path regex — a file whose relative path matches is skipped entirely (the escape hatch for "this extension but NOT under scripts/").

snippet_max.description: Max snippet length in the finding (long lines truncate).

type.description: Multi-pattern co-occurrence within one symbol's body span (e.g. exec + string concatenation in the SAME method). Needs parser-projected symbol spans; files without them are silently skipped.

file_pattern.description: Target file-path regex — the rule only scans matching files.

require_file.description: Cheap pre-skip: only scan a file whose whole text matches this regex. Absent = always scan.

require_file_all.description: Additional pre-skip regexes, ALL of which must match the file text.

require_file_absent.description: Negated pre-skip: if ANY of these matches the whole file text, the rule skips the file entirely.

skip_comment_lines.description: Skip comment lines (`//`, `*`, `/*` starts) when testing any pattern.

patterns.description: ALL of these must match somewhere within one symbol's body span for a finding.

trigger.description: The `patterns[].label` whose first (top-down) match supplies the finding's line and snippet.

trigger_in_loop.description: Structural gate: a trigger match only counts when it falls inside one of the file's projected loop spans (loop statement or array-iteration callback body). Files with no loop spans can never satisfy the trigger (graceful degrade).

absent.description: Vetoes: after every `patterns` entry is satisfied, the finding is dropped if ANY of these also matches a line in the SAME span (e.g. a guarding try/catch or `$transaction(...)` wrapper).

file_exclude_pattern.description: Path regex — a file whose relative path matches is skipped entirely.

snippet_max.description: Max snippet length in the finding (long lines truncate).

type.description: Query over a file's projected symbol declarations (naming-convention / banned-export style rules). Filters AND together. Runs in envelope mode too.

file_pattern.description: Target file-path regex — narrows the file set before symbol filters apply.

kind.description: Restrict to one symbol kind (zzop_core::SourceSymbolKind, lowercase serde form). Absent = any kind.

name_pattern.description: Regex on the symbol name — fires on a match, or on a NON-match under `negate: true`.

exported.description: Restrict to exported (`true`) or non-exported (`false`) symbols. Absent = both.

negate.description: Flips `name_pattern` from "must match" to "must not match" (e.g. "every exported component must be PascalCase"). With no `name_pattern`, every symbol passes.

type.description: Query over a file's projected IoFacts (boundary-convention rules, e.g. "every HTTP endpoint must be versioned"). Filters AND together. Runs in envelope mode too.

file_pattern.description: Target file-path regex — narrows the file set before io filters apply.

direction.description: Which side(s) of the file's IoFacts to query (zzop_core::IoDirection, lowercase serde form).

kind.description: Exact match against the io entry's kind string (open vocabulary — e.g. "http", "db-table", "env", "topic"). Absent = any kind.

key_pattern.description: Regex on the entry's normalized key — fires on a match, or on a NON-match under `negate: true`. An entry with an unresolved (null) key never matches; under `negate: true` that makes it a hit.

negate.description: Flips `key_pattern` from "must match" to "must not match".
